rule.desc: Use unchecked exceptions for programmer errors; use checked exceptions only when callers can realistically recover.
rule.rationale: Overusing checked exceptions increases boilerplate and hides root causes.
Guarantees deterministic cleanup and avoids leaks.

rule.desc: Wrap exceptions with a meaningful message and original cause.
rule.rationale: Preserves stack traces and root-cause analysis.

rule.desc: Catch the most specific exception type possible.
rule.rationale: Broad catches hide unrelated failures and reduce reliability.

rule.desc: Log errors at boundaries (API, job runner) and avoid re-logging the same exception.
rule.rationale: Duplicate logs obscure signal and inflate noise.

rule.desc: Validate external inputs at the boundary and fail fast.
rule.rationale: Prevents invalid data from propagating into core logic.
